name: Diabetic Chocolate Balls
recipeInstructions:
1. Sprinkle sweet granulated sugar substitute as needed.
2. Cream together margarine and sugar until light and fluffy.
3. Add vanilla and sugar substitute to the creamed mixture. Beat at medium speed for 30 seconds.
4. Stir together flour, cocoa, and salt to blend. Add to the creamed mixture and mix at low speed for about 1 minute or until blended.
5. Add nuts and raisins to the dough. Mix lightly.
6. Shape into balls using 1 tablespoon of dough per ball.
7. Place balls on a cookie sheet that has been lined with aluminum foil or sprayed with PAM spray.
8. Bake at 325°F for 20-25 minutes, or until slightly firm. Remove from oven and cool slightly.
9. Roll lukewarm balls in the sweet granulated sugar substitute. Cool to room temperature and serve two balls per serving.
Variation: Chocolate Mint Balls
Add 1/2 teaspoon peppermint flavoring along with 1 teaspoon vanilla instead of the 2 teaspoons vanilla.
